title: verify-attestation-tdx input
description: Input piped on stdin to `tinfoil-conformance verify-attestation-tdx`. Covers Intel TDX DCAP quote verification (Intel® TDX DCAP §2.3, §A.3/§A.4) and the Tinfoil SPEC §4 layer on top of it. The quote and collateral are passed inline so the SDK does NOT make network calls — the conformance binary wraps any network-fetching Getter with one that returns the injected collateral.

quote_b64.description: Standard base64-encoded raw TDX quote bytes (Quote Format v4 per Intel §A.3, or v5 per §A.4). The SDK parses the header to dispatch on version.

collateral.description: Quote verification collateral. Mirrors Intel's `tdx_ql_qv_collateral_t` (Appendix Quote Library Data Structures). All fields are required for the canonical verification path; SDKs may declare a capability if they require a subset.

tcb_info_json.description: Raw JSON string of Intel PCS TCB Info response (sample API: api.trustedservices.intel.com/tdx/certification/v4/tcb). The outer object is `{tcbInfo: {...}, signature: "<hex>"}` — SDKs extract the signature for chain verification and the inner tcbInfo for TCB level evaluation.

qe_identity_json.description: Raw JSON string of Intel PCS QE Identity response (sample API: api.trustedservices.intel.com/tdx/certification/v4/qe/identity). Outer object is `{enclaveIdentity: {...}, signature: "<hex>"}`.

pck_crl_der_b64.description: Standard base64-encoded DER PCK Certificate Revocation List (Intel PCS sample: pckcrl?ca=platform&encoding=der).

root_crl_der_b64.description: Standard base64-encoded DER Intel SGX Root CA CRL (certificates.trustedservices.intel.com/IntelSGXRootCA.der).

intel_root_ca_pem.description: Optional PEM-encoded Intel SGX Root CA certificate. If omitted, SDKs use their embedded copy (most SDKs `go:embed` or bundle it). Override exists for synthetic-root fixtures in later phases.

expiration_check_date_unix.description: Trusted current time (seconds since epoch) passed as Intel's `expiration_check_date`. Compared to X.509 NotAfter, CRL NextUpdate, and JSON nextUpdate fields. Per Intel §4.1.2 expiration alone does not fail the verification — it only sets `p_collateral_expiration_status`. SDKs surface this through the `collateral_expired` field on the outputs.

policy.description: Optional policy overrides on top of strict Intel verification. Phase 1 leaves this minimal; Phases 3 + 4 will populate.

accepted_qv_results.description: Quote verification result enum values acceptable to the relying party (Intel §B.1). Default policy is strict: only `"OK"` accepted. Permissive policies for non-terminal results (`SW_HARDENING_NEEDED`, `CONFIG_NEEDED`, `OUT_OF_DATE`, etc.) are exercised in Phase 3.

expected_fmspc_hex.description: Optional FMSPC pin. If set, the PCK leaf's FMSPC extension MUST match (rejects with PCK_FMSPC_MISMATCH otherwise).

tcb_evaluation_required.description: Default true. When false, the verifier skips Intel §4.7 collateral TCB/CRL evaluation (TCB Info match, QE Identity TCB level, PCK revocation, root revocation) and only verifies the cryptographic chain (PCK leaf → intermediate → root, AK binding, quote signature, QE report signature). Used by 'structural happy path' fixtures where the available testdata's collateral is intentionally out-of-sync with the quote's SVNs (e.g. google/go-tdx-guest's testdata, designed to test the TCB-rejection path).

expected_td_attributes_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.2. Exact-match pin on the TDATTRIBUTES (8-byte) field. Real-quote default is the Tinfoil-pinned policy (0000001000000000 = SEPT_VE_DIS only); fixture mismatches surface as TD_ATTRIBUTES_MISMATCH.

expected_xfam_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.1. Exact-match pin on XFAM (8-byte). Default expected per §4.8.6 is `e702060000000000`. Mismatch → XFAM_MISMATCH.

expected_mr_signer_seam_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.4. Exact-match pin on MR_SIGNER_SEAM (48 bytes). Current Intel TDX Module releases use all-zero. Mismatch → MR_SIGNER_SEAM_MISMATCH.

expected_seam_attributes_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.3. Exact-match pin on SEAM_ATTRIBUTES (8 bytes). All-zero for current Intel TDX. Mismatch → SEAM_ATTRIBUTES_MISMATCH.

expected_mrseam_allowlist.description: SPEC §4.8.5. List of allowed MR_SEAM hex values (48-byte each). Quote's MR_SEAM must match one. Empty list → MR_SEAM_NOT_ALLOWED for any value.

expected_mrtd_hex.description: SPEC §4.10. Exact-match pin on MRTD (48 bytes). Used to bind verification to a specific expected TD build. Mismatch → MRTD_MISMATCH.

expected_mr_config_id_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.6. Exact-match pin on MR_CONFIG_ID (48 bytes), default zero per §4.8.6. Mismatch → MR_CONFIG_ID_MISMATCH.

expected_rtmr3_hex.description: Cross-platform invariant from SPEC §7.3.6: RTMR3 MUST be all zeros for TDX. Mismatch → RTMR3_NONZERO.

expected_report_data_hex.description: SPEC §8.2. REPORT_DATA (64 bytes) typically binds the TLS public key (first 32 bytes = SHA-256 of TLS SPKI) and the attestation hash (bytes 32:64). Mismatch → REPORT_DATA_MISMATCH.

min_tee_tcb_svn_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.7. 16-byte minimum, compared element-wise against quote.tee_tcb_svn. Each byte of quote MUST be ≥ corresponding byte of minimum. Default per §4.8.6: `03010200000000000000000000000000`. Mismatch → TEE_TCB_SVN_BELOW_MINIMUM.

expected_qe_vendor_id_hex.description: SPEC §4.8.6. Exact-match pin on QE Vendor ID (16-byte UUID). Default: `939a7233f79c4ca9940a0db3957f0607` (Intel). Mismatch → QE_VENDOR_ID_MISMATCH.

enforce_spec_defaults.description: When true, applies SPEC §4.8.1/§4.8.2 normative checks regardless of expected_*_hex pins: DEBUG bit MUST be 0; TD_ATTRIBUTES bits outside FIXED0 (0|28|30|63) MUST be 0; XFAM bits 0+1 (FP, SSE) MUST be set; XFAM bits outside FIXED0 (0x0006DBE7) MUST NOT be set. Drives Phase 4B fixtures that test the canonical 'debug TD' and 'reserved-bit-set' cases.
